title: Ladle Refining Furnace (LRF)
author: JSL EKOS
description: This module explains the Ladle Refining Furnace (LRF), an important secondary refining process used to improve molten steel quality before continuous casting.
learningObjectives: Understand the purpose of the LRF process.
Learn how secondary refining improves steel quality.
Identify the major components of an LRF.
Understand alloy addition and temperature control.
Recognize quality and safety practices during refining.

content.introduction: The Ladle Refining Furnace (LRF) is used after the AOD process to refine molten steel further. It helps achieve the desired chemical composition, temperature, and cleanliness before the steel is sent for continuous casting.
content.purpose: The primary purpose of the LRF is to improve steel quality through temperature adjustment, alloy correction, desulphurization, inclusion control, and homogenization.
content.workingPrinciple: Molten steel inside a ladle is heated using electric arcs while argon gas is injected from the bottom. This promotes uniform mixing, temperature control, and efficient refining.
processSteps: Heat molten steel using electric arc.
Transfer refined steel to Continuous Casting.
safetyPrecautions: Maintain safe distance from molten steel.
content.summary: The Ladle Refining Furnace ensures that molten steel meets the required quality standards before casting. Proper refining improves cleanliness, composition accuracy, and casting performance.
